title: Shrimp Alfredo
description: Plump seasoned shrimp tossed with fettuccine in a velvety Parmesan Alfredo sauce. An indulgent restaurant-quality dinner.

ingredients.note: freshly grated melts much better than pre-shredded

ingredients.item: salt, white pepper, and pinch of nutmeg
salt and black pepper for shrimp
fresh parsley, chopped, and lemon wedges

steps.instruction: Cook pasta in generously salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water. Drain and set aside.
Season shrimp with garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add shrimp in a single layer. Cook 1.5–2 minutes per side until pink and just cooked through — do not overcook. Remove shrimp to a plate.
Reduce heat to medium. In the same pan, melt butter. Add garlic and sauté 1 minute until fragrant — do not let it brown.
Pour in heavy cream. Bring to a gentle simmer. Cook 3–4 minutes, stirring frequently, until the cream reduces slightly.
Reduce heat to low. Add Parmesan a handful at a time, stirring constantly after each addition until fully melted and smooth. Season with salt, white pepper, and a pinch of nutmeg.
Add drained pasta to the sauce. Toss well to coat. If the sauce is too thick, add pasta water 2 tbsp at a time until the sauce clings to the pasta.
Return shrimp to the pan. Toss gently to combine and reheat for 1 minute.
Serve immediately in warm bowls. Garnish with fresh parsley and a lemon wedge.

notes: Use freshly grated Parmesan — pre-shredded cheese has anti-caking agents that prevent it from melting smoothly into the sauce.
